hero_intro: I build AI systems that make it to production — RAG pipelines, multi-agent workflows, and ML models that hold up well outside the notebook.
summary: I'm a Machine Learning Engineer with 5+ years of experience designing, deploying, and maintaining production-grade ML systems, NLP pipelines, and cloud-native AI solutions. I currently build ML model services and REST APIs with PyTorch, Scikit-learn, and FastAPI at Apollo Global Management, after several years as a Data Scientist at Elevance Health building clinical risk and fraud-detection models. I hold an M.S. in Computer Science from the University of Central Missouri. I care about reliability, MLOps, and translating complex business problems into ML/AI solutions that hold up in production across AWS and Azure — not just in a notebook.

highlights: Specialization in Data Science and AI

description: Led design and delivery of AI/ML solutions across the full lifecycle, from statistical modeling and feature engineering to productionizing LLM-based agentic systems, partnering with investment teams and platform engineering to move solutions from prototype to production.

Built and deployed an ensemble forecasting model (XGBoost, Scikit-learn) on Azure Machine Learning, reducing forecast error (MAPE) by 11% and enabling more reliable data-driven portfolio planning across investment operations.

Designed and productionized RAG-based knowledge retrieval solutions using LangChain, OpenAI GPT, FAISS, Pinecone, and hybrid search architectures, reducing analyst research time from 15 minutes to under 6 minutes per request.

Architected multi-agent workflows using LangGraph and CrewAI with supervisor-based task routing and tool orchestration, automating multi-step research and reporting tasks and reducing manual coordination time by approximately 30%.
Implemented end-to-end MLOps workflows using Docker, Kubernetes, GitHub Actions and MLflow, automating model deployment, monitoring, and rollback processes across multiple production environments.

Built scalable ETL, feature engineering, and data validation pipelines using Apache Spark, Azure Data Factory, Airflow, and Azure Synapse Analytics, processing over 800GB of structured and unstructured data monthly while reducing manual preparation effort by 20%.
Built anomaly detection and risk-scoring models using ensemble learning algorithms, improving alert precision by 13% while maintaining regulatory compliance and governance requirements.
Applied SHAP, LIME, and explainable AI techniques to improve model transparency, support governance reviews, and accelerate validation cycles for business stakeholders.
Built monitoring dashboards using Tableau, Grafana, and Prometheus to track model performance, drift, infrastructure health, and operational KPIs, enabling proactive issue detection.

Developed healthcare utilization, claims forecasting, and predictive risk models using Python, SQL, Scikit-learn, XGBoost, and statsmodels, improving predictive performance (AUC) by 14% over legacy rule-based approaches and supporting earlier intervention decisions.

Built risk stratification and member propensity models using demographic, claims, and behavioral features, improving identification of high-risk populations by 16% and enabling more targeted care management interventions.

Developed NLP pipelines using BERT, spaCy, TF-IDF, and transformer-based architectures to extract insights from customer feedback and clinical narratives, reducing manual review effort by approximately 25%.

Automated model retraining, validation, and deployment workflows using Airflow, MLflow, and GitLab CI/CD, reducing model refresh cycles from five days to three days while improving reproducibility and reliability.
Processed and analyzed millions of healthcare claims, eligibility, provider, and member records using SQL, PySpark, and Hadoop to support enterprise analytics initiatives across business units.

Designed and analyzed A/B tests and hypothesis-testing frameworks to evaluate member outreach campaigns, improving engagement rates by 9% and informing rollout decisions for operational process changes.

Built Tableau and Power BI dashboards consumed by operational, clinical, and executive teams, enabling real-time visibility into program performance, model outcomes, and healthcare utilization trends.
Partnered with data engineers, care management teams, and healthcare stakeholders to operationalize machine learning solutions across clinical, operational, and financial analytics initiatives.

categories.name: Cloud Platforms
skills: Azure (Azure ML, Azure AI Foundry, Azure Synapse Analytics)
AWS (SageMaker, EC2, Lambda, S3, Glue, EMR, EKS, Redshift)

projects.title: Production-Grade Advanced RAG Tool
projects.description: A research-grade RAG system built with four architectural pillars: Pre-Retrieval, Advanced Retrieval, Post-Retrieval, and Self-RAG generation. Evaluated using a Tesla Owner's Manual corpus with RAGAS metrics tracked end-to-end in LangSmith. Implements HyDE, hybrid retrieval, parent-child chunking, cross-encoder reranking, and page indexing.

projects.title: HRMS MCP Server
projects.description: A production-ready MCP server for HR Management built with FastMCP and Groq API. Exposes HR workflows as callable tools including employee management, leave tracking, and organizational data queries. Leveraged Claude Code and prompt engineering during development to design tool schemas and system architecture. Designed to plug directly into any MCP-compatible host, making HR operations accessible to LLM-powered agents without custom API integrations.

projects.title: MCP Host Chat Interface
projects.description: A fully custom MCP host chat interface built from scratch using Node.js and the Groq API. Leveraged Claude Code for accelerated development, applied system design principles to architect the host layer, and used prompt engineering to control tool discovery, execution flow, and conversation behavior. Built as an independent alternative to Claude Desktop and Codex, giving full control over the MCP host layer with multi-turn conversation state managed entirely within a standalone interface.

projects.title: Portfolio Builder
projects.description: An open source portfolio website builder deployed on Vercel, built to help friends create and manage their own portfolio sites without writing code. Entirely designed and developed using prompt engineering, system design, and Claude Code — from architecting the Supabase schema and auth flows to building the admin and user dashboards, approval flows, and multi-theme system. Resend handles transactional emails throughout the platform. Made fully open source so anyone can self-host, contribute, or build on top of it.

projects.title: Credit Risk Prediction Model
projects.description: A machine learning classification model that predicts credit default risk based on borrower financial profiles. Applied feature engineering, class imbalance handling, and cross-validation to optimize precision and recall. Evaluated across multiple algorithms with XGBoost delivering the strongest performance on holdout data.

projects.title: Healthcare Premium Prediction
projects.description: A regression model that predicts individual health insurance premiums based on demographic and health features. Performed EDA to identify key premium drivers, applied feature transformations, and benchmarked multiple regression algorithms. Designed to support pricing and risk assessment workflows in healthcare.

projects.title: A/B Testing for Credit Card Launch
projects.description: A full A/B testing analysis for a new credit card product launch. Designed the experiment structure, defined control and treatment groups, and ran hypothesis tests to determine statistical significance of conversion and engagement metrics. Delivered data-driven recommendations on whether to proceed with the full rollout.

projects.title: E-Commerce Chatbot
projects.description: A semantic routing-based chatbot for e-commerce that classifies and routes customer queries intelligently. Uses embedding-based routing to handle product, order, and support intents without brittle rule logic.

certifications.name: Microsoft Certified: Azure AI Engineer Associate
